items.title: Tim Cook reactions: Trump, Altman, Buffett and more on the Apple CEO move
items.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook's move to executive chair spurred commentary from tech leaders Sam Altman and Palmer Luckey, as well as Warren Buffett.

items.title: Oil prices little changed as Trump expects Iran deal but prepared to resume war if talks fail
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate and Brent had settled 7% and 5% higher on Monday.

items.title: South Korea's Kospi hits record high amid mixed Asia markets as hopes linger for Mideast peace
items.summary: Korea's Kospi hits fresh intraday high, amid mixed Asian markets as investors brushed aside fresh threats from Trump on Iran ahead of a fragile ceasefire deadline.

items.title: Unemployment rate unexpectedly falls as fewer students look for work
items.summary: The drop has been largely driven by a rise in the number of people not actively seeking work.

items.title: 9 mid-cap stocks trading below their industry average PE
items.summary: A valuation scan highlights nine NSE midcap stocks trading below industry PE averages, including Hindustan Petroleum Corporation and Suzlon Energy, indicating potential undervaluation, though low PE may also reflect growth concerns or underlying business risks.

items.title: No decision yet on India unit listing; exploring strategic options to boost value: Pernod Ricard
items.summary: French spirits giant Pernod Ricard has stated no decision is made on listing its Indian subsidiary. The company is exploring options to enhance shareholder value. Pernod Ricard India is a leading alco-bev player. The company expects strong performance in India, its second-largest global market by value. India's premiumisation trends are driving growth.

items.title: Wall Street rises on AI optimism, strong corporate earnings
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.50%, the S&P 500 rose 0.19%, the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.25%

items.title: JioHotstar launches signal-led commerce advertising; Instamart signs on as first partner
items.summary: JioHotstar launches signal-led commerce advertising, an industry-first to engage high-intent consumers based on purchase signals, not just viewing habits. Quick commerce platform Instamart is the inaugural partner, leveraging this data-led approach to bridge content discovery with seamless purchasing during the IPL season.

items.title: Trump says Anthropic is shaping up and a deal is 'possible' for Department of Defense use
items.summary: Trump said his administration had "some very good talks" with Anthropic during a meeting at the White House last week.

items.title: U.S. stocks tick higher, led by UnitedHealth Group, as oil prices remain relatively stable
items.summary: U.S. stocks edged higher as UnitedHealth Group reported stronger-than-expected profits, nearing all-time highs. Oil prices remained stable amid cautious optimism that the U.S. and Iran will avoid an economic worst-case scenario, despite ongoing conflict and stalled negotiations.

items.title: UGRO Capital Q4 Results: Net profit jumps 26% YoY to ₹51 crore; income rises 51% YoY
items.summary: UGRO Capital reported a Q4 profit after tax of ₹51.1 crore, up 26% year-on-year. For FY26, PAT reached ₹174.8 crore, a 21% increase. The company aims to enhance MSME credit and achieved significant quarterly growth in focused verticals.

items.title: Youdha plans 200 outlets by year-end, bets big on India’s booming L5 passenger EV market
items.summary: Electric mobility brand Youdha is aggressively expanding its footprint by establishing over 200 retail outlets across India by year-end. This strategic move aims to bolster its presence in the burgeoning L5 passenger electric three-wheeler segment, catering to the increasing demand for last-mile mobility solutions nationwide.

items.title: Volkswagen announces voice AI in its Chinese cars from later this year
items.summary: Volkswagen revealed four cars, including the ID. UNYX 09, which the company said it co-developed with EV maker Xpeng in two years.

items.title: ReaRCo acquires KV Developers through the insolvency process
items.summary: ReaRCo Private Limited has acquired KV Developers, bringing relief to nearly 400 homebuyers in Greater Noida. The company secured Rs 195 crore from the SWAMIH Fund to finish the stalled project. Construction is underway on five towers, with possession expected by year-end. Dues to financial institutions and the development authority have been cleared.

items.title: Trump tells CNBC he expects U.S. to make ‘great deal’ with Iran
items.summary: President Donald Trump has repeatedly said he expects the U.S. and Israel war against Iran to end soon since it began in late February.

items.title: Treasury yields tick higher as markets await Iran peace talks and Warsh hearing
items.summary: Traders will also be monitoring Federal Reserve chair nominee Kevin Warsh's confirmation hearing on Tuesday.

items.title: Q4 Results Today Highlights: Nestle, Persistent Systems & 360 ONE WAM Q4 profit soar, HCL Tech, Tata Elxsi, Tata Investment, CMPDI to announce Q4 results, BoM, Groww, PNB Housing & Nelco shares surge
items.summary: Q4 Results Today, April 21, 2026, Highlights: Catch live updates

items.title: Family trusts cannot sponsor mutual funds, clarifies SEBI
items.summary: Regulator clarifies only body corporates are eligible as sponsors under existing mutual fund regulations

items.title: HCL Tech announces Rs 24 per share dividend, sets April 25 as record date
items.summary: HCL Technologies announced a Rs 24 interim dividend for FY27 with April 25 as record date. Q4 net profit rose 4.2% YoY to Rs 4,488 crore, while revenue grew 12% YoY, marking continued growth and consistent shareholder payouts.

items.title: Broker’s Call: Bank of Maharashtra (Buy)
items.summary: Systematix

items.title: RBI brings cross border transactions under e-mandate rules, allows customer to opt out anytime
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is enhancing digital fraud prevention. Cross-border recurring electronic payments via cards, prepaid instruments, and UPI are now under E-mandate rules. Customers can opt-out of transactions or mandates. Limits are set for recurring payments. Pre-transaction notifications are mandatory for most debits. These rules aim to protect customers and ensure secure electronic transactions across borders.

items.title: Brits fled to Dubai for low taxes — now war is making some rethink the deal
items.summary: The U.K. is seeking to lure British residents back from the UAE as war in the Middle East rattles confidence in Dubai’s safe‑haven image.

items.title: Uni staff strike for cost of living 'weighting'
items.summary: Library, museum, finance and IT staff are among members of Unite taking action over pay.

items.title: Cyient DLM Q4 Results: Profit falls 28% to Rs 22 crore
items.summary: Electronic Manufacturing Services (EMS) firm Cyient DLM on Tuesday reported 27.7 per cent decline in consolidated net profit at Rs 22.44 crore for March quarter FY26.

items.title: European stocks lower as U.S.-Iran ceasefire deadline looms
items.summary: European stocks traded broadly lower on Tuesday as investors try to gauge the direction of efforts to end the U.S.-Iran war.

items.title: The US cattle industry is eating its own tail
items.summary: From rising temperatures to water shortages, ranchers need to be thinking about a plan B

items.title: HCL Tech Q4 Results: Cons PAT up 4% YoY at Rs 4,488 crore, revenue rises 12%; Rs 24 per share dividend declared
items.summary: HCL Tech Q4 Results: IT major HCL Technologies on Tuesday reported a 4.2% growth in its consolidated net profit for the March-ended quarter at Rs 4,488 crore versus Rs 4,307 crore in the year ago period. The profit after tax (PAT) is attributable to the owners of the company.

items.title: HCL Tech Q4 Results: Board declares interim dividend of ₹24 per share. Details here
items.summary: HCL Technologies announced an interim dividend of ₹24 per share for FY26, marking its 93rd consecutive quarter of payouts. The record date is April 25, with payments on May 05. The company reported Q4 FY26 revenue of ₹33,981 crore, reflecting growth in both QoQ and YoY terms.

items.title: HCL Tech Q4 results: Profit rises 4% YoY to ₹4,488 crore; revenue up 2.4% YoY in CC
items.summary: HCL Tech Q4 results: Consolidated profit rose by 4% YoY to ₹4,488 crore, while revenue from operations for the quarter under review grew by 12.35% YoY to ₹33,981 crore.

items.title: Quotes of the day by Bill Miller: “All of the great investing periods begin when things are terrible and end when they are wonderful.”
items.summary: Legendary investor Bill Miller highlights that prime investment opportunities emerge during market downturns, not when things appear rosy. Periods of fear often present undervalued assets, while widespread optimism can signal market peaks. Successful long-term investing hinges on emotional discipline, a contrarian approach, and unwavering patience, allowing investors to capitalize on overlooked prospects and avoid the pitfalls of herd mentality.

items.title: Shell faces fresh climate court case
items.summary: Dutch non-profit activist group makes push to halt new oil and gas production

items.title: Akshaya Tritiya jewellery buying turns more prepaid and offer-led, led by tier-2 and tier-3 demand
items.summary: Akshaya Tritiya 2026 saw tier-2 and tier-3 cities lead jewellery sales growth. Prepaid transactions dominated purchases. Consumers favored lightweight gold jewelry, balancing cost and aspiration. Digital-first brands gained prominence. Beauty emerged as a top category. UPI and financing options facilitated purchases. This signals a lasting change in Indian festive shopping.

items.title: India, Singapore charting out roadmap for digital corridor, green shipping: official
items.summary: India and Singapore are collaborating on a roadmap for digital corridors and green shipping infrastructure. This initiative aligns with global decarbonisation efforts in the maritime sector. Details regarding investment, fuel demand, and facilities are being finalized. The partnership leverages India's green energy resources and Singapore's maritime hub status. This collaboration aims to develop new opportunities for both nations.

items.title: Gainers & Losers: Nestle, Groww and Ola among 7 big movers on Tuesday
items.summary: Seven major stocks—including Nestle, Groww, Lemon Tree Hotels and Ola—logged sharp moves on Tuesday as markets rallied for a third straight session, driven by strong earnings, sectoral buying and stock-specific triggers.

items.title: Chair nominee Kevin Warsh says Fed must ‘stay in its lane’ to maintain independence
items.summary: Warsh also expressed firm commitment to fighting inflation with only one mention of the labor market.

items.title: Clean energy generation surges in India, coal still dominates: Report
items.summary: India's renewable power generation reached a record high in 2025. Solar and wind power led this surge, setting new individual records. This growth led to a decline in coal generation. However, coal still remains the primary source of electricity in India. This trend highlights a significant shift in the country's energy landscape.

items.title: US Stock Market Today | Dow Jones | Nasdaq Live: US stocks edge higher as Iran ceasefire deadline nears
Amazon coerced Levi's, Hanes, big brands to inflate product prices on rival retail websites: Antitrust lawsuit filing
items.summary: The filing is part of the 2022 antitrust lawsuit, which alleged that Amazon artificially inflated the cost of products to shield its profit margins.

items.title: Nestlé India Q4: Volume boost drives best sales growth in a decade; profit jumps 26%
items.summary: Nestlé India reported a net profit of ₹1,114 crore. Revenue climbed 22.6% to ₹6,747.7 crore. The company focuses on consumer-centric growth and expanding its rural market reach.

items.title: Broker’s Call: Mastek (Buy)
items.summary: The company will see better growth in FY27 vs FY26, despite AI-led pricing pressure and macro uncertainty; The US saw sequential growth in order-book for the second quarter in a row

items.title: US stock market today: Dow, S&P 500 futures rise up to 0.6% as AI optimism offsets geopolitical uncertainty
items.summary: US stock futures indicate a positive start as optimism rises for US-Iran peace talks. Key indices are trading higher by 0.3%–0.6%. Investors focus on potential negotiations while tech stocks lead a strong market recovery, with Nasdaq up 17% in April.

items.title: Swiggy Instamart logs 49x gold surge on Akshaya Tritiya with small-ticket gold driving quick-commerce boom
items.summary: Swiggy's Instamart experienced a massive jump in demand for gold and silver on Akshaya Tritiya. Urban shoppers embraced quick commerce for auspicious purchases, opting for smaller gold denominations. Silver saw high-value transactions, with carts even combining precious metals with groceries. This highlights a new trend in festive shopping.

items.title: Nifty notches third straight win, FMCG and Realty lead the charge
items.summary: The day’s gains came on the back of a broader cocktail — improving global sentiment around potential US-Iran peace negotiations, easing crude oil prices, and a strong start to the fourth-quarter earnings season

items.title: Electricity bills targeted in planned shakeup to energy pricing
items.summary: The war in the Middle East has brought renewed attention to Britain's vulnerability to energy price shocks.

items.title: UnitedHealth tops quarterly estimates, hikes profit outlook as insurer manages high medical costs
items.summary: The nation's largest private insurer expects 2026 adjusted earnings of more than $18.25 per share, up from a previous outlook of greater than $17.75 per share.

items.title: India’s GDP growth to slow to 6.4% in FY27 amid geopolitical headwinds: UN report
items.summary: The report, by the United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific, projected that India’s GDP growth would pick up moderately to 6.6% in FY28, driven by domestic demand and services.

items.title: Global palm oil supply to tighten in FY27 amid biodiesel push and weather risks: SEA
items.summary: In fiscal year 2027, the outlook for palm oil production across the globe appears precarious. Major exporting nations are intensifying their biodiesel initiatives, stripping away palm oil for local energy applications. This trend, paired with the potential El Nino effects on Indian rainfall patterns and fluctuating global political tensions, is likely to limit the availability of exportable palm oil.

items.title: Moody’s Ratings cuts India’s FY27 growth forecast to 6% on higher energy costs
items.summary: Moody's has lowered India's FY27 growth forecast to 6% due to weaker private consumption and industrial activity, driven by higher energy prices from the Iran war. Elevated energy costs are expected to widen the trade deficit and increase fiscal pressures, though government infrastructure spending and strong forex reserves offer some support.

items.title: Billionbrains Garage Ventures (Groww) shares rally 9% on strong Q4 profit surge
items.summary: Investors reacted positively to the earnings momentum and growth outlook

items.title: BoM shares jump over 6% on strong Q4 earnings
items.summary: The stock rose over 6% on the NSE, up from ₹75.54, due to strong investor response to the bank’s earnings and growth outlook

items.title: Lessons from Hormuz crisis: Vedanta’s Anil Agarwal shares how India can break the 88% crude dependency cycle
items.summary: Recent Strait of Hormuz tensions reveal India's deep dependence on imported oil. Vedanta Chairman Anil Agarwal stresses India is under-explored, not resource-poor. He advocates for faster exploration and investment, suggesting India could hold vast untapped hydrocarbon potential. Agarwal believes technology and global partnerships are key to reducing import reliance and securing India's energy future.

items.title: UltraTech crosses 200 MTPA capacity, set to become world’s largest cement maker outside China
items.summary: UltraTech Cement has achieved a significant milestone, crossing 200 million tonnes per annum of installed cement manufacturing capacity in India. This expansion positions the company as the world's largest cement producer outside China. The company's capacity has grown rapidly, adding the next 100 MTPA in under seven years. Future plans include further expansion to over 240 MTPA.

items.title: Trump tariff refunds begin but consumers likely to miss out
items.summary: Businesses can apply online through a portal for refunds expected to total $160bn.

items.title: Stock Market Strategy: Is it time to raise exposure in equities? What should investors do?
items.summary: Indian equity markets fell over 11% in March due to the US-Iran conflict and rising oil prices. DSP's report suggests this correction offers a chance for investors to increase equity exposure as valuations approach long-term averages, particularly in large-cap stocks.

items.title: Vedanta subsidiary Hindustan Zinc announces board meet date to consider Q4 results, interim dividend. Check details
items.summary: Hindustan Zinc, a Vedanta group company, has announced its fourth quarter earnings meeting on April 24, 2026. Analysts expect significant profit growth driven by rising zinc prices, with sales projected to increase by 34.6% year-on-year.

items.title: Persistent Systems Q4 Results: Profit jumps 34% YoY to Rs 529 crore, revenue up 25%
items.summary: Mid-tier IT services company Persistent Systems on Tuesday reported a 33.73 per cent growth in consolidated net profit to Rs 529.26 crore in the January-March quarter of FY26.

items.title: After Triumph, KTM, Bajaj joins 350cc shift: Pulsar NS400Z gets new engine
items.summary: Bajaj Auto has updated the Pulsar NS400Z with a new 350cc engine, aligning with industry trends driven by GST benefits. This move, following similar updates from KTM and Triumph, aims to make performance motorcycles more accessible. The revised 349cc engine offers slightly reduced power but maintains the bike's existing features and pricing.

items.title: Royal Mail to ask part-time posties to work more to meet letter targets
items.summary: Royal Mail has faced a chorus of criticism for failing to meet its targets on letter delivery.

items.title: Aurobindo Pharma announces schedule, record date for its Rs 800 crore share buyback
items.summary: Aurobindo Pharma has announced an Rs 800 crore share buyback via the tender route, offering Rs 1,475 per share. The buyback opens April 23 and closes April 29, with a higher entitlement for retail investors. The move signals a capital deployment strategy and management confidence despite its modest impact on equity.

items.title: Nippon India Mutual Fund launches investor awareness campaign to boost retail participation
items.summary: Nippon India Mutual Fund has launched 'Har Ghar Investor,' an awareness campaign to boost retail investor participation and promote disciplined investing. The initiative aims to encourage at least one household member to invest in mutual funds, addressing a significant gap in the market.

items.title: Textile, seafood stocks in focus after US tariff refund roll-out, select counters end higher
items.summary: Avanti Feeds and Apex Frozen Foods settle lower after hitting their 52-week highs on profit-booking sales

items.title: Revolut aims for $200bn valuation in stock market listing
items.summary: Bumper IPO would trigger increased stake for founder Nik Storonsky but group has no plans to float shares before 2028

items.title: ‘New cards on the battlefield’: U.S., Iran ratchet up rhetoric with peace talks in limbo
items.summary: American negotiators may be outmatched at the negotiating table by Iran's experienced diplomatic delegation, says analyst, ahead of potential high-stakes meeting.

items.title: ICICI Prudential AMC, two others pay ₹14.35 lakh to SEBI to settle regulatory violation
items.summary: The Scheme was constituted with an initial tenure of four years commencing on September 26, 2013, with a provision for two extensions of one year each, SEBI noted in its order dated April 16

items.title: Quick-commerce, Nescafe coffee push and Ad spends: Inside Nestle India's growth play
items.summary: Nestle India achieved its best quarterly results in ten years. This strong performance was fueled by significant volume increases across its products. The company saw robust growth in its e-commerce and beverage segments. Increased advertising spending also played a key role in driving sales. Nestle India maintained healthy profit margins through efficient operations and strategic investments.

items.title: Bank of Maharashtra among 7 midcap stocks that hit 52-week high; rally up to 32% in a month
items.summary: Nine BSE MidCap stocks hit fresh 52-week highs during a strong market rally, led by gains in metals, financials and industrials. The surge reflects robust momentum and investor confidence amid a broader upswing in equities. Such breakouts are typically viewed as bullish signals, indicating potential continuation of upward price trends in select stocks.

items.title: Crypto investment trend: Millennials, Gen X lead adoption signalling market participation shift in India — What we know
items.summary: There is a surge in crypto adoption among older millennials and Gen X in India, with Uttar Pradesh and Maharashtra leading. The 35+ demographic is now the fastest-growing segment indicating shift towards focus on long-term investment, as per a CoinSwitch report.

items.title: $12 billion Trump tariff refund for Indian exporters. Which stocks may benefit?
items.summary: Tariff refunds are set to begin after the US Supreme Court ruled Donald Trump’s tariffs unconstitutional. US Customs and Border Protection has launched the first phase of its refund system, allowing importers to file claims—potentially benefiting export-focused companies.

items.title: Krafton, Naver Corporation launch ₹6,000 cr India-focused unicorn growth fund
items.summary: The Fund will be managed and advised by Mirae Asset Venture Investments (MAVI) -- the Mirae Asset group's private investment platform

items.title: How the energy shock has reshaped jet fuel economics
items.summary: Also in today’s newsletter, environmental groups sue to block BP’s $5bn deepwater drilling project

items.title: India builds insurance shield for risky sea trade as global cover dries up
items.summary: In response to the challenges posed by renewed hostilities affecting maritime trade, Indian public sector insurers have unveiled an innovative maritime risk-sharing pool. This strategic initiative aims to support sea trade to conflict-stricken areas, stepping in where international insurers have retreated.

items.title: Stock Market Highlights: Stock markets climb nearly 1% amid hopes for progress on Iran-US peace talks
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Price Highlights: Stock markets advanced for the third consecutive day on Tuesday, with Sensex jumping by 753 points and broader Nifty closing above 24,550 following a drop in crude oil prices and hopes for progress on peace talks between Iran and the US.

items.title: US waiver set to keep Russian oil exports to India near record high
items.summary: India shipped in a record 2.25 million barrels ⁠per day of oil from Russia in March, nearly double February's volumes, making Russian oil 50% of its imports

items.title: Traders! Stay long, trail your stops as Nifty rally broadens, says Vinay Rajani; picks 3 stocks
items.summary: The Indian market is recovering with broad participation. Technical analyst Vinay Rajani sees the Nifty moving towards 24,800. He highlights JSW Steel for a breakout, Jubilant FoodWorks for a reversal pattern, and Marico for its resilience. Traders are advised to stay invested and trail their stop losses as the market advances.

items.title: Another flight leaves passengers behind due to border delays
items.summary: New European border rules have caused delays at airports across the continent, affecting flights.

items.title: Denim, not discounts: Why Ace Turtle is moving up the value chain
items.summary: Ace Turtle is shifting away from the value denim segment to focus on premium brands such as Lee and G-Star. It expects 25% revenue growth this fiscal, driven by expansion in tier-II and -III cities, while investing in AI to improve operational efficiency.

items.title: Rupee falls, tracking Asian peers; partial rollback of RBI FX curbs weighs
items.summary: The Indian rupee logged its ​steepest fall in a ​week on Tuesday, tracking weakness in most regional ​peers and also weighed down by the partial rollback of measures the central bank had undertaken recently to support the strained South Asian ‌currency.

items.title: Multibagger hunter Vijay Kedia buys 4.5% in SME Mahamaya Lifesciences; adds smallcap SPML Infra in Q4
items.summary: Ace investor Vijay Kedia boosted his smallcap exposure in Q4 by acquiring 4.53% in Mahamaya Lifesciences and 1.88% in SPML Infra. Kedia, known for spotting multibaggers early, added these picks as both firms show improving momentum, aligning with his long-standing preference for SME and niche growth stories.

items.title: Milk supply, prices remain stable amid West Asia crisis; no disruption in dairy operations: Govt
items.summary: "Milk procurement processing and supply across the country have remained uninterrupted amid the ongoing West Asia crisis. Price of milk and milk products are stable. No supply disruptions have occurred in the market and payments to dairy farmers have continued throughout the crisis period," said Rustagi during an inter-ministerial briefing.

items.title: Zoho built a global SaaS business from Tamil Nadu, can startups move beyond metro hubs?
items.summary: Zoho has built a global SaaS business from Tamil Nadu without venture funding or metro hubs. As startup funding tightens, its model focused on internal talent development and distributed teams is drawing renewed attention, though replication remains limited.

items.title: Nestle India shares jump 8% to new record high. What changed after Q4 results?
items.summary: Nestle India shares surged over 8% to a record high after the company posted strong Q4 FY26 results, with net profit rising 27% YoY to Rs 1,111 crore and revenue up 23%. Robust volume growth, higher advertising spends, and strong performance across key brands boosted investor sentiment.

items.title: Rupee plunges 32 paise to settle at 93.48 against US dollar
items.summary: Positive domestic equity markets failed to boost local currency, which also had some impact of the Reserve Bank's latest move to ease curbs on speculative bets in non-deliverable forward markets

items.title: Mint Explainer | Apple names new CEO: Decoding Cook’s tenure, Ternus’s prospects
items.summary: A hardware veteran at the helm signals Apple’s device-first strategy even as AI reshapes Big Tech.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on April 21: Nestle India, Groww, PNB Housing, Bank of Maharashtra, Sobha among top gainers
items.summary: On April 21, the Indian stock market closed higher due to optimistic investor sentiment linked to potential peace talks involving Iran. The Nifty 50 and Sensex rose, with notable gains in FMCG, Realty, and Private Bank sectors, while Nifty Pharma lagged slightly.

items.title: Snap, Disney, Meta: Over 81,200 layoffs in 2026 - Is AI push risking your tech job security?
items.summary: A major catalyst driving these layoffs is restructuring linked to AI, with tech firms increasingly directing funds towards automation and efficiency even as it leads to trimming the workforce.

items.title: Nifty can rally to 30,089 in Prabhudas Lilladher’s bull case scenario, adds 3 stocks to model portfolio
items.summary: Domestic brokerage Prabhudas Lilladher sees Nifty rallying 24% to 30,089 in a bull-case scenario, driven by strength in banks, capital goods and telecom. It added Polycab India, JSW Steel and Fortis Healthcare to its model portfolio, while cautioning on inflation risks and global uncertainties.

items.title: India flips AB InBev from witness to target in antitrust probe, triggering court fight
items.summary: India's antitrust agency has shifted Anheuser-Busch InBev from a cooperating witness to a party under investigation in a cartel probe. The investigation, initially targeting 42 alcohol retailers in Telangana for allegedly excluding rivals, saw AB InBev raided in 2024. The brewer obtained a temporary court injunction halting the investigation against it.

items.title: Counterfeit Mounjaro case: Eli Lilly issues statement after Gurugram drug racket exposed
items.summary: Gurugram's law enforcement has taken a bold step by breaking up a ring creating counterfeit Mounjaro injections, with two individuals now in custody for their role in the illicit trade of diabetes and obesity medications. The illicit enterprise involved importing raw materials from China and manufacturing the injections in a private home.

items.title: Sensex jumps 753 points, Nifty 50 ends above 24,550; investors earn ₹3 lakh crore in a day; US-Iran peace talks in focus
items.summary: The Sensex rose by 753 points, or 0.96%, to end at 79,273.33, while the Nifty 50 ended with a healthy gain of 212 points, or 0.87%, at 24,576.60.

items.title: China’s silver imports jump to record high. Is it time to buy silver?
items.summary: China imported around 836 tons of silver in March, marking a record high driven by retail demand and the solar sector. This surge is significantly above the 10-year average of 306 tons, but high import levels may not be sustainable long-term.

items.title: EU countries struggle to replenish gas reserves for winter
items.summary: Iran war keeps prices high despite consumer demand dropping as heating season ends

items.title: Delhi-NCR, Bengaluru office rentals cross Rs 100/sq ft/month milestone: Knight Frank
items.summary: Office rental rates in Bengaluru and Delhi-NCR have now crossed Rs 100 per sq ft. This milestone is driven by strong demand for prime workspaces that outstrips available supply. Mumbai already sees rentals above this mark. Across eight major Indian cities, office rents saw a 2-15 percent annual increase in early 2026. Leasing activity reached a record 29.

items.title: A rush of price hikes at companies as margins take priority over volumes
items.summary: From cement and paints to FMCG, companies are taking calibrated price hikes to offset crude-led cost pressures—but weak demand and inflation risks threaten the margin-volume balance.

items.title: India should aim for 100% ethanol blending, says Nitin Gadkari
items.summary: India aims for 100 percent ethanol blending soon. This move is crucial for energy self-reliance amid global oil market uncertainties. The country currently imports 87 percent of its oil. Efforts are underway to boost alternative and bio-fuel production. Green hydrogen is identified as a future fuel.

items.title: US-Iran peace talks: Why could oil prices stay above $85/barrel even if Strait of Hormuz opens? Explained
items.summary: Brokerages estimate crude oil prices to remain high even if the blockade at Strait of Hormuz opens. Domestic brokerages have pegged oil prices to average at $85 per barrel in the near term.

items.title: PL Capital sees Nifty at 27,080; flags geopolitical risks, crude-led pressures
items.summary: PL Capital said the impact of higher crude may be relatively contained compared to past shocks due to lower oil intensity of GDP

items.title: What is happening to gas and electricity prices?
items.summary: Typical household bills fell by 7% when the new energy cap took effect on 1 April 2026.

items.title: Multiples, Warburg among PE giants in race for $100-mn Giva stake
items.summary: Giva’s fundraise comes amid rapid growth in India’s jewellery market, particularly the lab-grown diamond segment, valued at $300–350 million in 2024 and expected to grow at a 15% CAGR over the next decade, according to Redseer.

items.title: Rolex Rings shares gain 17% as board to consider buyback proposal on THIS date
items.summary: Rolex Rings shares rose 17% to ₹163.24 after announcing a board meeting to discuss a buyback proposal on April 23, 2026. The stock has gained 46% in April, recovering losses from March despite still trading 41.4% below its record high.

items.title: Adani Power incorporates nuclear arm to deepen atomic energy push
items.summary: Adani Power has established a new company, Rawatbhata-Raj Atomic Energy. This move expands its involvement in nuclear power generation. The new entity is a wholly owned subsidiary of Adani Atomic Energy Ltd. This development signals Adani's commitment to its atomic energy plans. India is increasing its nuclear capacity with private sector involvement.

items.title: Resolven inks 25-year power supply pact with SECI for upcoming 180-MW wind energy project in Andhra Pradesh
items.summary: Clean energy producer Resolven has signed a significant agreement with Solar Energy Corporation of India. This pact will supply electricity from a new 180-megawatt wind energy project. The project is expected to generate substantial clean energy annually. This will power many Indian households and reduce carbon emissions.

items.title: Marine fuel blenders, refiners chase heavy sweet oil amid Iran war disruptions
items.summary: The reduced availability of fuel ⁠blending components is occurring at the same time refiners are reducing low-sulphur fuel oil production for marine fuel because of crude shortages, which is supporting spot premiums for the residual fuel used to power ships

items.title: Indian plastic maker Alternicq says Iran war has pushed up costs
items.summary: The Iran conflict has sent plastic prices soaring by 40 percent, impacting the cost of routine commodities such as bottled water. This increase is no small matter, as manufacturers are compelled to pass these costs onto consumers. The silver lining? Analysts believe that, should peace return, costs may return to normal in four to six months.

items.title: India, South Korea aim to deepen ties amid geopolitical uncertainty. Here is what's holding them back.
items.summary: India and Korea find alignment as U.S.–China tensions reshape trade — but struggle to move beyond intent

items.title: Is the US stock market due for a deep correction amid stretched valuations and geopolitical risks?
items.summary: With the S&P 500 nearing all-time highs, experts debate whether the US stock market is primed for a correction. Rising inflation, geopolitical tensions, and stretched valuations present risks.

items.title: Collapsed law firm at centre of £39.5m fraud probe
items.summary: Sheffield-based PM Law Ltd, which had 25 offices across England, closed suddenly on 2 February.

items.title: Petrol thefts surge as Iran war pushes up fuel costs
items.summary: One petrol retailer says he is experiencing about five drive-offs a week at each forecourt, costing him thousands.

items.title: Nestle Q4 Results 2026: Net profit rises 26% at ₹1,114 crore; declares final dividend of ₹5/share
items.summary: Nestle reported a 26% increase in Q4 net profit, reaching ₹1,114 crore, up from ₹885 crore last year. The company announced a final dividend of ₹5 per share for the 2025-26 financial year.

items.title: Nestle India Q4 Results: Cons profit jumps 27% YoY to Rs 1,111 crore; Rs 5/sh dividend declared
items.summary: Nestle India Q4 Results: Nestle India reported a robust 27% year-on-year rise in consolidated net profit to Rs 1,111 crore for the March quarter, exceeding market expectations. Revenue from operations also saw a significant 23% jump to Rs 6,748 crore.

items.title: MF Picks: 13 smallcap stocks surge up to 115% in six months; 3 turn multibaggers
items.summary: MFs are ramping up exposure to smallcaps, with 13 picks soaring up to 115% in six months and three turning multibaggers.

items.title: FedEx completes India's first intra-city drone delivery flight trials in Bengaluru
items.summary: FedEx has successfully completed India's first intra-city drone delivery flight trials in Bengaluru, in collaboration with IIT-Madras. These trials, conducted under the FedEx SMART Centre research framework, demonstrated a significant reduction in transit time, cutting a 60-minute road journey to just 21 minutes via a 39-42 km aerial route.

items.title: Atomgrid opens Bengaluru R&D centre to tap 'China-plus-one' shift in chemicals sector
items.summary: Atomgrid proudly announces the launch of a state-of-the-art research and development centre in Bengaluru, signaling a significant step towards addressing the increasing worldwide demand for chemical supply chains that operate independently of China. This innovative facility is set to specialize in developing cost-efficient and creative solutions tailored for global consumers.

items.title: Hindustan Copper plans Rs 7,189-cr capex over 5 years for expansion of mines
items.summary: Hindustan Copper Limited is investing over Rs 7,000 crore in the next five years to expand its mines. Production capacity is set to significantly increase. The company is also undertaking a major digital transformation. This includes upgrading communication systems and implementing advanced technologies like 5G and AI.

items.title: Wipro share buyback: Should you participate or not in ₹15,000-crore offer? Experts weigh in
items.summary: Wipro buyback: IT giant Wipro announced its biggest-ever share buyback on April 16, worth ₹15,000 crore. Check out buyback details, along with expert opinions on whether you should participate or not.

items.title: Gold Price Outlook: Is the yellow metal still the most trusted asset for Indian households?
items.summary: Gold remains a key asset for Indian households despite an 8% price drop due to geopolitical tensions. The trend towards financial investments, such as ETFs, shows trust in gold is evolving rather than declining. Strong demand from central banks and domestic flows support a positive outlook for gold.

items.title: CleanMax to supply RE power to Shell from upcoming 30 MW hybrid projects
items.summary: CleanMax will power Shell's Gujarat LNG terminal and Karnataka technology centre. The company is building hybrid renewable energy plants in both states. Gujarat will receive power from a 16.83 MW solar and wind project. Karnataka's 13.2 MW hybrid plant will supply its Bengaluru technology centre. This initiative enhances energy stability for Shell's operations.

items.title: Sebi’s crackdown on options frenzy is bearing fruit, NSE data shows
items.summary: Small investor participation in the high-risk equity options segment dropped by nearly 25% in FY26 as tighter regulations and higher entry barriers began to rein in excessive speculation by retail investors.

items.title: Quitting tobacco could give economic boost to over 20.5 million households in India
items.summary: Imagine a future where millions of Indian families break free from the grasp of tobacco, climbing up the economic ladder. The most disadvantaged, particularly those in rural areas, will see the greatest advantages, while middle-income households can also experience considerable growth. This reveals the substantial economic incentives tied to quitting tobacco.

items.title: Penny stock under Re 1 jumps over 8% after this acquisition update
items.summary: Penny stock under Re 1: NHC Foods share price jumped 33% in one month, and gained 12% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis. The penny stock has fallen 5% in six months, but gained 3% in one year.

items.title: Late-night crypto trading rises in India, dip-buying seen during corrections: CoinSwitch
items.summary: A report by CoinSwitch shows Indian investors are most active in crypto trading between 10–11 PM, reflecting a preference for post-work hours. Despite 24/7 markets, weekday trading dominates, indicating a shift toward more disciplined, routine-driven investing.

items.title: Mehul Telecom IPO Day 3: Issue subscribed over 21x so far; NIIs steal the show
items.summary: Mehul Telecom IPO opened on April 17 and closes April 21, offering shares at ₹96 to ₹98. The company focuses on mobile devices and aims to raise ₹28 crore, with a listing planned for April 24 on the BSE SME platform.

items.title: Groww's Ishan Bansal sees structural shift in derivatives participation post regulatory changes
items.summary: Derivatives business faces a structural reset post FY25 regulatory changes, with growth now driven by a smaller but stable customer base, increased market volatility, and an evolving product mix. Despite a dip in equity derivatives participation, future growth is anticipated from overall industry expansion and increasing per-user activity as younger investors engage more.

items.title: Infra.Market founders take on debt to pump more cash into firm as IPO plans slow
items.summary: Building materials platform Infra.Market’s founders are raising personal debt via a holding entity to inject equity into the firm, as IPO timelines stretch and valuation pressures mount.

items.title: FSSAI directs food manufacturers to use only Ashwagandha roots, extracts; not leaves
items.summary: Food safety regulator FSSAI has issued a directive to all food businesses. Only Ashwagandha roots and their extracts are permitted for use. The use of Ashwagandha leaves in any form is now prohibited. This clarification comes after some manufacturers were found using the leaves. The Ayush Ministry has also issued similar instructions.

items.title: Sensex gains 1,300 points in 3 sessions: Can de-escalation in the US-Iran war drive it to 85K?
items.summary: The Indian stock market is buzzing with optimism as analysts predict a significant rise in the Sensex and Nifty 50, fueled by potential peace talks between the US and Iran. Can a final deal see Sensex soar to 85,000?

items.title: Trump tariff refunds begin today: Gokaldas Exports, Avanti Feeds, other textile and shrimp stocks rally up to 7%
items.summary: Export-oriented shrimp and textile stocks rallied up to 7% on Tuesday after the US began rolling out refunds for tariffs imposed during Donald Trump’s presidency, following a Supreme Court ruling that struck them down. Companies like Gokaldas Exports and Avanti Feeds saw strong gains as investors bet on improved US demand and stability ahead.

items.title: Japan scraps ban on lethal weapons exports in major shift of pacifist policy
items.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said that "no single country can now protect its own peace and security alone."

items.title: Prestige Estates sells over 1,700 housing units in Hyderabad for more than Rs 2,500 crore
items.summary: Prestige Estates Projects Ltd has achieved a significant sales milestone in Hyderabad. The company sold more than 1,700 housing units for over Rs 2,500 crore. This success comes from their recently launched residential project, Prestige Golden Grove. The project has seen strong demand since its launch.

items.title: JPMorgan expands $1.5 trillion economic security splurge into Europe
items.summary: The banking giant is looking to invest in areas deemed critical to economic security, including defense and AI.

items.title: Stock market holiday: Why is the Israeli stock market closed today?
items.summary: Israel’s Memorial Day begins at sundown on April 20, marked by ceremonies, a nationwide siren, and restricted public activities. It will transition into Independence Day festivities, including fireworks and parades.

items.title: UN agency prepares Hormuz evacuation plan for hundreds of ships
items.summary: The International Maritime Organization is preparing an evacuation plan for hundreds of ships stranded in the Persian Gulf after weeks of conflict involving U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran. Secretary General Arsenio Dominguez said the plan will be activated only if tensions ease, with vessel departures prioritised by crew conditions.

items.title: $166 bn Trump tariff refunds open in US, but India still has to ask for a slice
items.summary: A significant reduction in US tariffs is setting the stage for a $166 billion refund wave. For Indian exporters, the potential for profit exists, but their success will depend on how well they can negotiate with American partners. Importantly, while US importers can claim refunds, it's up to Indian businesses to strategically navigate their commercial agreements to recoup expenses.

items.title: India bond clearing house said to apply for European recognition
items.summary: The Clearing Corp. of India Ltd. sent an application earlier this month to the European Securities and Markets Authority for recognition under European market infrastructure guidelines

items.title: Sensex jumps 1,300 points in three days ahead of Iran war ceasefire expiry: 4 key factors
items.summary: Indian benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty surged nearly 1% on Tuesday, extending gains for a third consecutive session. Cooling oil prices, hopes of Iran-US peace talks, and a global market rally fueled the optimism. Trent shares led the gains, while broader markets also saw positive movement.

items.title: Global markets face rising strain as supply chain tightens and oil risk persists: Neelkanth Mishra
items.summary: Global supply chains are showing stress as inventories run dry. This is impacting manufacturing and even international aviation. Oil markets face short-term volatility with long-term questions about resilience. India's energy exposure is a temporary risk, with a focus on electrification. The global economy operates within a narrowing window of resilience.

items.title: Trent shares jump 8% in two days as investors await bonus issue bonanza on April 22. What to expect?
items.summary: Trent shares surged after the company announced a board meeting to consider a bonus issue, dividend and fund-raising plans. The stock has gained sharply in recent sessions, supported by strong business momentum. Despite expected pressure on operating profit, analysts remain focused on the growth outlook driven by store expansion and steady revenue performance.

items.title: Lemon Tree Hotels shares soar 13%. What’s keeping traders active?
items.summary: Lemon Tree Hotels' shares surged over 13% following the announcement of its expansion into Gujarat with a new hotel signing. This move, part of a broader asset-light growth strategy, reinforces the company's focus on tourism and pilgrimage markets. The company is also undergoing a strategic restructuring with Warburg Pincus to unlock shareholder value.

items.title: Ceasefire uncertainty keeps gold prices steady as crude surges on Hormuz closure
items.summary: Spot gold was trading around $4,794–$4,825 per ounce, pinned within its recent $4,750–$4,850 band, while silver hovered near $79.30, holding above the key $78 support level

items.title: Global stocks have recouped Iran war losses to hit fresh records — but the shadow of Mideast conflict still looms
items.summary: Global stocks have clawed back losses triggered by the Iran conflict, with major indexes now trading at or above pre-war levels.

items.title: Sensex surges 700 points at midday; Trent, Bajaj Finance lead rally on ceasefire optimism
items.summary: Broader market breadth remained firmly positive on the BSE. Of 4,274 stocks traded, 2,667 advanced against 1,418 declines, with 189 unchanged.

items.title: Global Markets | South Korea's KOSPI rises to record high as chipmakers surge on export boom
items.summary: South Korea's KOSPI index reached an all-time high, surpassing its previous peak, driven by a surge in chipmakers following robust export data. Semiconductor sales saw a significant increase, boosting investor sentiment ahead of key corporate earnings reports. The market is closely watching upcoming results from major players like SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics.

items.title: Tea café chain Chaayos in talks to raise $50-70 million, appoints banker
items.summary: The development comes nearly four years after the company raised $53 million in its Series C funding led by Alpha Wave Ventures. The chain was valued at about $250 million following its round in 2022, according to Tracxn data.

items.title: MFs raise stakes in ICICI Bank as FPIs trim exposure in FY26
items.summary: Mutual fund holdings climbed to 32.42% as of March 31, 2026, up from 29.86% a year earlier

items.title: Global Markets | Australian shares end flat on US-Iran peace talk uncertainty; Rio Tinto gains
items.summary: Australian shares saw little movement as investors awaited U.S.-Iran peace talk developments. Mining giant Rio Tinto bucked the trend, rising on strong first-quarter output. Meanwhile, tech and real estate sectors showed recovery in April after significant March quarter losses. Across the Tasman, New Zealand's inflation held steady at 3.1%.

items.title: Coforge launches two AI solutions for airlines
items.summary: The announcement comes as Coforge shares trade at ₹1,294.20 on the NSE, up 0.47 per cent on the day, with the stock recovering from a 52-week low of ₹1,008.10 hit in March 2026

items.title: Musk and insiders to retain voting control of SpaceX after IPO, filing shows
items.summary: Upon completion of the offering, Musk will stay on as chief executive officer, chief technical officer, and will serve as chairman of SpaceX’s nine-member board of directors

items.title: J.P. Morgan lifts S&P 500 year-end target to 7,600 on AI-driven earnings
items.summary: The ⁠revised target implies an upside of about 6.9% from Monday's close of 7,109.14. The Wall Street brokerage had cut its ‌target to 7,200 from 7,500 last month

items.title: The 20-somethings juggling three jobs to make ends meet
items.summary: While the UK unemployment rate stands at 4.9%, increasing numbers of those in work have more than one job.

items.title: Global Markets | Japan's Nikkei ends higher as tech gains on Middle East deal optimism
items.summary: Japan's Nikkei index saw gains on Tuesday. This rise was driven by hopes of peace talks between Tehran and Washington. Investors bought major technology companies. Chip-related stocks performed well. However, some experts warn of potential market corrections due to supply chain disruptions. Banking shares and Toyota Motor experienced declines.

items.title: Small-cap FMCG stock inches close to 52-week high. Do you own?
items.summary: GRM Overseas shares rose over 2% after announcing its launch of 10X Basmati Rice Suitable for Diabetics. The product aims to meet rising demand for healthier food options and supports the company's strategy to expand its health-focused portfolio in domestic and international markets.

items.title: HCL Tech Q4 Results Highlights: PAT jumps 4% YoY to ₹4,488 crore; ₹24 dividend announced
items.summary: HCL Tech Q4 Results Highlights: HCL Tech posted a 4.20% year-on-year (YoY) rise in Q4 net profit (attributable to owners of the company) to ₹4,488 crore. The figure stood at ₹4,307 crore in the same period last year.

items.title: 'No theme runs forever': Devina Mehra busts the ‘buy and forget’ investing myth
items.summary: Devina Mehra warns investors against the behavioral trap of assuming past market leaders will continue to dominate. She highlights the importance of adapting to market changes and avoiding the flawed 'buy and forget' strategy.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Tim Cook finds the new Apple of his eye
items.summary: Apple announces its second leadership change since Steve Jobs, with CEO Tim Cook set to be succeeded by John Ternus at the world's second most valuable company.

items.title: Betting on large banks, cautious on housing finance and microfinance; watch guidance in FMCG: Sandip Sabharwal
items.summary: Earnings season is here. Sandip Sabharwal sees banks as a strong sector. He prefers large private banks due to asset quality. IT is steady but not exciting. FMCG companies face rising costs. Real estate offers selective value. Housing finance and microfinance are areas to avoid for now. Sabharwal's view is constructive with caution.

items.title: Suzlon eyes Europe comeback with next-gen wind turbines
items.summary: The Pune-based company on Tuesday launched two new turbine designs for the European market with capacities of 5 MW and 6.3 MW, based on its new Blue Sky product platform. These add to its portfolio of 2.1 MW and 3 MW machines.

items.title: From crude to monsoon risks: Indian consumer demand splits under inflation pressure
items.summary: Lower-income households are cutting back, while affluent consumers turn cautious, with the divide beginning to show up in FMCG and durables demand.

items.title: Aluminium recyclers push for duty cut as supply tightens globally
items.summary: India, heavily reliant on scrap imports, faces disruptions due to geopolitical tensions and potential EU export curbs

items.title: West Asia conflict clouds outlook, but infra investments seen rising 45–50% through FY27–28: Crisil
items.summary: India's infrastructure investment is set for strong growth of 45-50% over the next two years. This surge is fueled by robust domestic demand and supportive government policies. Sectors like renewable energy and data centers will lead the expansion. While the West Asia conflict may cause indirect cost pressures, overall investment remains on track.

items.title: Nifty 50 Trading Strategy: Analysts recommend Bull Call Spread options strategy for 28 April expiry
items.summary: Nifty 50 Trading Strategy: Axis Securities has recommended a Bull Call Spread strategy for Nifty options contracts expiring on 28 April 2026, forecasting a moderately bullish view.

items.title: Gold falls as investors await clarity on US-Iran talks, dollar edges up
items.summary: The US dollar edged up on Tuesday, ⁠making greenback-denominated commodities more expensive for holders of other currencies.

items.title: Apple incoming CEO John Ternus faces a defining challenge: Fixing the company's AI strategy
items.summary: Tim Cook had a highly successful tenure as Apple's CEO, but he leaves his successor with a big gap to fill when it comes to the company's position in AI.

items.title: Beyond zero: Soft drink makers double down on 'functional' fizz, zero calories and local flavours
items.summary: As zero-sugar becomes a market baseline, brands turn to probiotics, vitamins and 'modern-traditional' flavours to win the ₹30,850 crore category.

items.title: Ola Electric shares surge 70% in April on sales revival. Are they a better bet than rival Ather?
items.summary: After falling for six months in a row, Ola Electric's stock price has surged 70% in April so far, boosted by an increase in vehicle registrations. Meanwhile, rival Ather Energy shares have inched higher by 19% in April so far.

items.title: India's March crude imports slide on Iran war, Russian volumes hit record high, data shows
items.summary: Traffic at the Strait of Hormuz, ⁠which typically handles roughly one-fifth of the world's oil supply, has come to a standstill after Iran and the United States blocked vessels from passing

items.title: Groww share price soars 10% to hit record high after Q4 profit jumps 122%: Is it the right time to buy?
items.summary: Billionbrains Garage Ventures, parent of Groww, saw shares rise 10% to ₹216 after a strong Q4FY26, reporting a 122% YoY profit increase to ₹686 crore and 87% revenue growth. User activity and new product segments like Margin Trading contributed to overall success.

items.title: MSCI boots Indonesian tycoon-owned stocks from indices
items.summary: Index provider delays decision on whether to downgrade south-east Asia’s largest economy to frontier market

items.title: Vodafone Idea, Groww, Bank of Maharashtra among the most traded stocks today, 21 April 2026
items.summary: Most traded stocks today: Vodafone Idea, Billionbrains Garage Ventures (Groww), Bank of Maharashtra, Lemon Tree Hotels, Ola Electric Mobility, Suzlon Energy, YES Bank, and Reliance Power were among the most traded stock on the NSE.

items.title: India faces inflation spillover risks from Middle East conflict: RBI
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has issued a cautionary statement regarding escalating inflation rates. Tensions in the Middle East are causing significant disruptions in global supply chains and energy sectors, potentially embedding price volatility within the economy. Instead of slashing demand, the bank aims to guide expectations judiciously.

items.title: New era as Apple names new boss to replace Tim Cook after 15 years
items.summary: Ternus will take over running the technology giant in September as Cook steps up to become executive chairman.

items.title: Jio Financial shares slip after Q4 profit falls 14%; stock down 20% YTD
items.summary: The Reliance Industries-backed financial services company reported a consolidated net profit of ₹2.72 billion for the March 2026 quarter, down 14% from ₹3.16 billion a year earlier

items.title: Indian debt funds cut hedges as oil risks inflate rate-hike bets
items.summary: The two-year swap rate is still trading near 6%, about 40 basis points above levels before the Iran conflict, showing markets continue to price in higher-for-longer interest rates

items.title: 9 Stocks to Watch: Suzlon, Hyundai, TVS Motor, HCL Tech, TCS, JSW Steel
items.summary: India–Korea business ties expanded with multiple MoUs across renewable energy, steel, IT and mobility sectors, as Suzlon, TVS Motor, Hyundai, JSW Steel, TCS and others announced major global partnerships, investments, expansions and technology collaborations

items.title: Elevated foreign ownership a positive for Indian financial firms: Fitch
items.summary: Foreign investors are showing increased interest in Indian financial firms. Fitch Ratings notes this can bring long-term capital and better governance. Transactions focusing on internal controls and risk management are key. Investors seek scalable platforms with local expertise. This trend could strengthen standalone credit profiles for institutions like Manappuram Finance and SMFG India Credit.

items.title: Pop-up shop helps families cope with rising costs
items.summary: Customers receive tokens to claim up to 10 free items, from clothing to everyday home essentials.

items.title: Bonds are not buying it

items.title: Vedanta shares hits 52-week high as demerger record date triggers rally
items.summary: The stock opened sharply at ₹791.70 before pulling back slightly, with traded volume already at 109.55 lakh shares and turnover crossing ₹860 crore

items.title: India's economy projected to grow at 6.4% this year: UN
items.summary: India’s economy is projected to grow 6.4% in 2026 and 6.6% in 2027, according to a United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific report, after expanding 7.4% in 2025 on strong consumption and tax cuts.

items.title: High foreign ownership positive for Indian financial institutions: Fitch
items.summary: Fitch said recent greater interest from foreign investors indicates their rising confidence in India's long-term growth prospects, the financial sector's regulations and oversight and improved risk governance

items.title: Charity offers 'stigma-free' food poverty service
items.summary: The Devon charity shop lets food bank users select items rather than be given pre-prepared parcels.

items.title: 'Supercharger' call for threatened ceramics firm
items.summary: A petition is calling for struggling Denby Pottery to be added to a government support scheme.

items.title: 'Price of red diesel is putting us in the red'
items.summary: Lincolnshire grower says rising costs have forced her to reconsider her son's nursery fees.

items.title: Quality and branding: The twin pillars of India’s global horticulture push
items.summary: A coordinated push involving policy support, private investment, and technology adoption can help India shift from volume-driven to value-led horticulture by fixing productivity, post-harvest, and market gaps, say experts.

items.title: Nvidia supplier Victory Giant sees shares soar 60% in Hong Kong debut
items.summary: Shares of Victory Giant Technology surged in what is Hong Kong's largest initial public offering debut so far this year.

items.title: Garment exporters see demand recovering as US tariff pressures ease
items.summary: Indian garment exporters expect a strong business rebound in the second half of the fiscal year. Easing US tariff pressures are bringing buyers back. Orders for the first two quarters were booked at peak tariffs. Revenue improvement will be visible from October. Exporters are diversifying markets to Europe and Africa.

items.summary: Wall Street banks have started trading credit default swaps against private credit funds run by Blackstone, Apollo and Ares

items.summary: Amazon will invest up to $25 billion in Anthropic, which plans to spend over $100 billion on Amazon's cloud tech. Details here.

items.summary: Brent slips below $95 as traders await clarity on US–Iran ceasefire extension and peace talks, even as Strait of Hormuz tensions and naval seizures heighten supply risks.

items.summary: Company code-named Project Prometheus is working on models for industrial applications

items.title: India, US continue to have 'positive, productive discussions' on trade deal
items.summary: United States and India are nearing a trade agreement. Negotiations in Washington DC show positive progress. Senior officials indicate most key issues are settled. The focus is now on resolving minor differences. This development marks a significant step in economic ties between the two nations. An announcement could be made soon.

items.title: Mint Explainer | Tata turmoil: A snapshot of simmering tensions at the group
items.summary: Internal rifts, legal challenges, and a debate over going public have sparked a power struggle within the group following the death of Ratan Tata.

items.title: Champagne raises a toast to growth in India, duty cuts to add to the fizz
items.summary: Under the proposed India-EU free trade agreement, duties on champagne are set to fall from 150% to 20% over seven years.

items.title: After years of distress, Kota's Resonance nears debt deal with 80% haircut
items.summary: Following the covid disruption, Kota’s coaching ecosystem experienced a prolonged period of closures and uncertainty. While legacy players such as Resonance attempted to adapt through online classes, competition intensified from new-age edtech entrants

items.title: Pocket FM's microtransactions, AI use in content creation are driving growth, COO Gangwar says
items.summary: Pocket FM's innovative approach to audio content through microtransactions and AI integration has led to remarkable growth. The platform's ability to retain users and compete in a crowded market showcases its potential for future expansion.

items.title: How Amazon allegedly used Levi's and Hanes to force rivals to raise prices - What California court filing reveals
items.summary: California's attorney general says price fixing by Amazon is caught 'explicitly and egregiously in writing.' Read what the newly unsealed court documents reveal about how it worked.

items.title: Jio Financial to leverage marketplace for high-risk products, credit cards
items.summary: Jio Financial Services Ltd chief executive officer Hitesh Sethia said the company plans to use its newly-launched marketplace to sell products that are otherwise not within its risk appetite.

items.summary: John Ternus, Apple's 50-year-old head of hardware engineering, will become the technology giant's chief executive officer on 1 September 2026, the company announced on Monday. Cook, 65, will move into the newly defined role of executive chairman

items.title: IPL teams appeal for tax rematch: 'Treat event as sport, not entertainment'
items.summary: Indian Premier League teams are asking the government to reclassify match tickets. They want IPL games treated as sports, not entertainment. This could cut the goods and services tax on tickets significantly. The current 40% tax is seen as too high for sporting events. Teams believe this change will benefit the sports ecosystem and its growth.

items.summary: Apple said on Monday that John Ternus is succeeding Tim Cook as CEO, with Cook assuming the role of executive chairman on Sept. 1.

items.title: Rick Perry’s Fermi Is Undermining the AI Energy Thesis
items.summary: The Fermi Paradox asks why, given the vastness of the universe, there is no hard evidence of aliens. The Fermi Inc. paradox asks why, given seemingly insatiable demand from hyperscalers for electricity, former Energy Secretary Rick Perry’s AI-energy firm hasn’t obtained a hard power contract from said hyperscalers.

items.title: RBI plans to remain nimble to prevent persistent supply shock amid West Asia crisis
items.summary: Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra addressed global challenges at Princeton University, stressing upon agile monetary policy to counter supply shocks from West Asia. The focus is on preventing inflation from becoming persistent. Prudent fiscal management and domestic production also play key roles, he said.

items.title: Deal activity value jumps 18 pc to USD 123.8 bn in 2025; volumes drop marginally
items.summary: India's deal activity saw a significant rise in 2025. Deal values increased by 18 percent to USD 123.8 billion. This growth occurred even as the number of transactions decreased. Investors focused on larger, strategic deals in infrastructure and financial services. Cross-border deals also saw a substantial value increase. This indicates continued investor confidence in the Indian market.

items.title: US oil refiners reap windfall from Iran war
items.summary: Soaring fuel prices and access to cheap North American crude oil put American refiners in pole position

items.title: Trump’s social media posts have transformed oil trading, says Citadel
items.summary: Hedge fund’s commodities head says traders are struggling to adjust to volatility sparked by president’s frequent messages

items.title: CBI arrests two RCOM senior officials in bank 'fraud' case
items.summary: The two executives were important functionaries of the Reliance Communications group, managing corporate finance, banking operations, payments/utilisation of funds, etc.

items.title: Kash Patel sues The Atlantic for $250 million over alcohol abuse claims
items.summary: The Atlantic article said FBI Director Kash Patel "has alarmed colleagues with episodes of excessive drinking and unexplained absences."

items.title: Phase-I of bilateral trade agreement with US almost ready: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: Trade conversations are heating up in Washington as India and the US engage in crucial discussions aimed at enhancing economic ties. At the forefront are negotiations on preferential market access that could allow for a more favorable entry of Indian products into the American market.

items.title: India, South Korea working to double bilateral trade by 2030: Piyush Goyal
items.summary: Both countries have been tasked by their leadership to raise two-way trade from the current $27 billion to $54 billion by 2030, says commerce minister Piyush Goyal

items.title: India's core sector contracts 0.4% in March, hurt by West Asia conflict
items.summary: The index of eight core industries, which tracks coal, crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, fertilisers, steel, cement and electricity, accounts for 40% of the Index of Industrial Production (IIP), a broader measure of factory output in the economy.

items.title: Investors are misreading news about the Iran war, analysts say as markets whipsaw
items.summary: Monday's declines put the brakes on another rapid recovery in global stock indexes akin to last year's 'liberation day' bounce.

items.title: Jack Daniel's owner eyeing shake-up? Decades-old distiller favours Pernod amid struggling US spirits market - Report
items.summary: With the potential tie-up, Brown-Forman could reportedly have a better route to market Jack Daniel’s in China and India – where demand for whiskey is rising, given Pernod's wide distribution network

items.title: Govt doubles down on wheat exports, clears extra 2.5 million tonnes
items.summary: The decision is expected to enhance market liquidity, facilitate efficient stock management, and prevent distress sales during the peak arrival season, the government said on Monday.

items.title: 'Resumption of hostilities': Seized ship, vessel attacks push U.S.-Iran ceasefire toward brink
items.summary: A U.S. seizure of an Iranian cargo ship and reports of vessels coming under fire in the Gulf have pushed a fragile ceasefire with Tehran to the brink.

items.title: The luxury of hindsight, the urgency of foresight: What India can still get right
items.summary: Iran war: Recent West Asian events highlight India's vulnerabilities in energy, trade and finance. Foresight was dismissed, but now hindsight offers lessons. India must prepare for future disruptions by institutionalising scenario planning and risk assessment. This proactive approach is crucial for navigating global pressures and maintaining policy space.

items.title: Postal Department revenue jumps 16% YoY to ₹15,296 crore, says Union Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia — Check details
items.summary: The Department of Post has registered an annual revenue of ₹15,296 crore for FY26, up 16% YoY, Union Communications Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia said today on 20 April, calling it a “historic” year.

items.title: Testing the waters? Why Airtel is raising the tariff of a high-value prepaid plan
items.summary: The telecom major’s latest price tweak may be a calculated gamble to gauge subscriber ‘stickiness’ before broader, industry-wide hikes, experts told Mint.

items.title: Widening CAD a concern, says Niti Report
items.summary: In its quarterly Trade Watch Report for the quarter to December 2026, released on Monday, it said the instability in West Asia has slowed the India-Gulf Cooperation Council Free Trade Agreement (FTA), affecting trade diversification and market access. "FTAs are not a one way street, nor should they be, which is to say that in the way that we are seeing them as a tool for market access, others are seeing it as a tool for market access too," said NITI Aayog vice chairman Suman Bery.

items.title: Core sector output fell 0.4% in March, lowest in 19 months
items.summary: India’s core sector output contracted 0.4% year-on-year in March, marking its lowest level in 19 months, driven by a sharp decline in fertiliser production amid the West Asia conflict and weaker performance across energy-related industries, according to official data released on Monday.

items.title: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
items.summary: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

items.title: Washington-backed rare earth group to buy Brazilian miner for $2.8bn
items.summary: USA Rare Earth’s deal for Serra Verde highlights America’s push to secure access to critical minerals

items.title: RBI draft for upper layer non-banks affects CICs disproportionately, raises compliances costs
items.summary: New Reserve Bank draft on upper layer non-bank finance companies disproportionately impacts core investment companies by increasing compliance costs. Mandatory listing requirements could prove onerous for many CICs, particularly those not seeking public market access. The AUM-based approach may present challenges for these entities, especially regarding listing and governance.

items.title: Govt reviews rural schemes, supply chain woes to protect beneficiaries from Iran war impact
items.summary: The Rural Development Ministry is reviewing its key schemes in response to the West Asia conflict's potential impact on global supply chains and prices. The ministry is ensuring uninterrupted employment and timely fund flow for beneficiaries. Measures are being taken to protect rural housing and road projects. This includes timely fund releases and material bank creation.

items.title: Certus Capital to invest ₹2000 cr in housing, mixed-use projects in FY27
items.summary: The push comes amid a surge in capital flows into Indian real estate, with investments hitting a record $14.3 billion in 2025, up 25% year-on-year

items.title: FY27 capex growth of states pegged at 8–10%: report
items.summary: State capital spending growth is set to slow in FY27. This moderation follows a strong FY26. Rising revenue expenses and slower revenue growth are key factors. Geopolitical issues in West Asia could further impact state finances. Fiscal discipline will be crucial for states balancing welfare and investment needs. Top states continue to prioritize infrastructure development.

items.title: India’s core sector growth contracts 0.4% in March 2026 to lowest level since August 2024
items.summary: March 2026 brought unexpected challenges for India's foundational industries, as the Index of Eight Core Industries reported a contraction of 0.4 percent. The sharp declines in essential sectors such as fertiliser, crude oil, coal, and electricity contributed significantly to this downturn, reversing the positive momentum that had been evident just a month prior.

items.title: Women take the lead in whiskey as more female drinkers and distillers change the industry
items.summary: Women take the lead in whiskey as more female drinkers and distillers change the industry

items.title: India growth resilient, markets near correction: HDFC Securities
items.summary: India's economy is set for robust growth. Real GDP is projected at 6.5 percent for FY26-FY27. Nominal GDP will expand between 10 to 11 percent. Inflation is expected to ease to 4.5 percent. Fiscal deficit targets are set at 4.3 percent. Retail investor participation remains strong. Markets may be nearing the bottom of the current correction cycle.

items.title: India, South Korea to upgrade trade pact, deepen tech and supply chain ties
items.summary: India and South Korea will resume talks to upgrade their economic partnership agreement. The goal is to increase bilateral trade to 50 billion dollars by 2030. Both nations will also enhance cooperation in critical technologies and supply chains. An economic security dialogue will be established. Discussions will cover shipbuilding, steel, and sustainability. Industrial collaboration will expand through new agreements.

items.title: Dharavi project begins; 11,000 rehab homes being constructed on railway land in Matunga
UP allows wheat sale at MSP without online registration
items.summary: Uttar Pradesh farmers can now sell wheat at government procurement centers without prior online registration. This decision comes after many farmers faced difficulties registering and were forced to sell to middlemen. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has ordered immediate implementation. Farmers can now sell their wheat as they did in previous years, ensuring fair prices for their produce.

items.title: Middle East crisis as it happened: ‘Highly unlikely’ Iran ceasefire will be extended if no deal reached, Trump says
items.summary: US delegation expected to leave ‘soon’ for Pakistan peace talks even as Tehran’s attendance remains unclear

items.title: Yes, tokens do have real uses in finance
items.summary: Tokenisation is taking hold in areas including money, bonds, gold, funds, real estate and private equity

items.title: The looming battle over the Fed’s balance sheet
items.summary: What will Warsh choose?

items.title: US stablecoins pose danger to emerging markets, say central bankers
items.summary: Growth of dollar-pegged crypto assets under Donald Trump risks accelerating dollarisation and enabling criminal misuse

items.title: Wealth advisers made billions from private capital fees
items.summary: Plus, RIP Mark Mobius, BlackRock cools on Europe and Jasper Johns in Bilbao

items.title: Private credit gets the once-over
items.summary: Do you want the bad news or the bad news?

items.title: South Korea's Lee to seek big boost in economic ties in summit with PM Modi
items.summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung is in New Delhi for talks with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi. The visit aims to significantly increase economic ties, focusing on shipbuilding. Both nations plan to nearly double bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030. Cooperation may also expand into finance, artificial intelligence, and defense. India's role in global production is highlighted.

items.title: Inflation anxiety may have made a comeback, especially for low-income groups
items.summary: The latest survey by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) reveals growing inflation anxiety, with households’ perceptions and expectations of inflation rising. These perceptions are usually influenced by the food and fuel price outlook.

items.title: India’s affluent consumers spend more, but retail’s share is shrinking
items.summary: Affluent Indians are shifting spending toward experiences, shrinking retail’s share even as premium purchases rise. Retailers are adding immersive formats to keep up.

items.title: Why your recycled clothes could end up in this South American desert
items.summary: Old garments from around the world are being discarded in the South American country.

items.title: Finance ministry may review MOOWR benefits for battery storage imports
items.summary: The Finance Ministry is set to discuss customs warehousing benefits for imported battery storage systems. Industry groups have raised concerns about the MOOWR scheme creating an uneven playing field. Consultations with power and renewable energy ministries are planned. The government is examining potential misuse and policy gaps. A decision will align with India's energy security goals.

items.title: Gig workers social security scheme may have multiple fund managers
items.summary: The labour ministry of India is charting a new course by investigating a multi-manager framework for its latest social security initiative aimed at gig and platform workers. The EPFO is likely to manage the provident fund components, while various fund managers will look after pension allocations.
